item.title: I found heaven!
item.description:
Flew up to Portland, OR for a friend's bday. This is where he wanted to spend it. Looks pretty nondescript from the outside.
Apparently this is a pinball museum in Hillsboro, OR, just about 30 minutes outside Portland.
Oh this is not just a museum. $20 entrance fee but then you get freeplay for everything inside.
So much amazing stuff inside. Apparently this is the world's largest lunchbox collection, among other memorabilia.
Tons of figures and other collectibles mint in box
Posters and stills, VHS covers, album covers, pretty much everything pop culture
Everything on free play!
One of the few things that actually costs money. I did not wish to be big.
Over 450 pinball machines and arcade cabinets in total. I could have spent the entire weekend in here and still not played everything!
The famous KISS pinball machine!
Mini cabinets for the little ones!
It is a real game now! I did not find a Sugar Rush Racing though
If you like Star Wars, this place is worth a visit too!
Another little Star Wars corner
Finally got a 4 player experience with TMNT! So much easier on freeplay. Though I did want to ice my arm afterward.
We finally beat TMNT! 10 year old me would be so happy!   If you are ever in the area, or willing to make a trip, check it out. https://www.nextlevelpinballmuseum.com edit: Viral and Staff Pick? WOW! If you want to see what else we're up to, give us a follow on Twitch. https://www.twitch.tv/d33kode we do a weekly TTRPG stream of a game we're designing (cyberpunk/supernatural mashup) every Thursday at 8pm Pacific.
item.dc:creator: swhertzberg

item.title: Secret village discovered
item.description: A SECRET VILLAGE DISCOVERED In 1850, a Farmer Found a Secret Village Older Than The Great Pyramids of Egypt ‘In a small bay in Scotland, a well-kept secret is hidden among the green hills. For millennia, no one knew that this place ever existed. Time and weather buried it under the sand, but when a terrible storm swept over the Orkney Islands in 1850, an incredible secret was revealed... Within this rolling hillside is a perfectly preserved prehistoric village of Skara Brae. At first glance, it might not seem particularly impressive, but step inside and you'll be amazed at what you see. Thousands of years ago, it was a bustling society. The settlement consists of eight stone houses and was inhabited between roughly 3180 and 2500 B.C., making Skara Brae one of the oldest agriculture villages in the UK. Skara Brae has been called the “Scottish Pompeii” because the ancient monument is so well preserved. Archaeologists estimate that 50-100 people lived in the village. When the settlement was built, the houses were 1,500 meters from the sea. Now, the sea has dug closer to the village and the view from the settlement has changed from pastures to the sea. The houses were connected to each other by tunnels. Each house could be closed off with a stone door. In every room, there's always one bed bigger than the other, but no one knows why. Each room also contains cabinets, dressers, seats, and boxes for storage. These boxes were waterproof, suggesting that they might have stored live seafood for later consumption. The village also had a sewage system and each house had its own toilet. Some believe that the villagers were Picts, a people of unknown origin who settled in eastern and northern Scotland near the end of the British Iron Age. But archaeological findings have shown that the people who lived here could have lived been much earlier than that. And no one knows why the village was abandoned. But around 2500 B.C., ​​the Orkney Islands became cooler and wetter. Many theories speculate about how the people of Skara Brae met their fate; the most popular ones involve a violent storm.’
item.dc:creator: rippinro

item.title: If I want a crocodile woman to have some fat fuckin' honkers, there's nothing you can do to stop me.
item.dc:creator: ThunderBashie

item.title: I have a hormonal coil/IUD and it wasn't due a change for another year never had a problem in the four years I've had it
item.description: Living in England I found out I can literally get sent the pills by the post with just an over the phone consultation and then they send them. My boyfriend and I found it really hard to make this decision, it was a shock but we decided we want to buy a house and be stable prior to having a child and we relied on my coil working. I'm kinda heartbroken but I know it's for the best, I'm just so thankful to live somewhere where I don't have to go through the torture that American women have to go through. I'm so thankful to have the choice and will fight for other women to have their choice too.
item.dc:creator: nettiegreen

item.title: Peeling apart some mica found while rockhounding
item.description: Sound on for soothing river noises Front page edit: Folks are asking me these questions a lot so I'm gonna add an edit to answer them. 1. Where is this from? Spruce Pine, North Carolina! 2. How are your nails so nice? I take multivitamins for strength and only use a file to shape and shorten them, never clippers. I also use a cuticle lotion from Lush called Lemony Flutter. This was also very early in the trip, so I hadn't had much chance to mess them up yet. 3. What is mica? It's a mineral that forms in thin sheets like this, made from silicate and potassium. It's non-toxic and commonly used in make-up and other cosmetics.
item.dc:creator: InsightfulLinguist
